title: Barry Diller trusts Sam Altman. But ‘trust is irrelevant’ as AGI nears, he says.
description: Barry Diller defended OpenAI CEO Sam Altman, while warning that AGI remains an unpredictable force needing guardrails.

title: Snap says its $400M deal with Perplexity ‘amicably ended’
description: The deal, announced last November, would have seen Perplexity's AI search engine integrated directly into Snapchat.

title: Is xAI a neocloud now?
description: xAI's real business may be more about building data centers than training AI models.

title: How Elon Musk left OpenAI, according to Greg Brockman
description: Cutthroat negotiations between startup founders are rarely shared so publicly, especially when a company becomes as world-changing as OpenAI.

title: SpaceX may spend up to $119B on ‘Terafab’ chip factory in Texas
description: The project would be a "multi-phase, next-generation, vertically integrated semiconductor manufacturing and advanced computing fabrication facility," according to the proposal.

title: DeepSeek could hit $45B valuation from its first investment round
description: The Chinese AI lab came to prominence in early 2025 after launching a large language model that trained on a fraction of the compute power and at a fraction of the cost of the big U.S. models like those from OpenAI and Anthropic.

title: Google updates AI search to include quotes from Reddit and other sources
description: While citing web forums and discussion boards can help users find answers to more niche queries, this design choice could also prove chaotic.

title: Khosla-backed robotics startup Genesis AI has gone full stack, demo shows
description: Genesis AI, a startup that raised a $105 million seed round to build foundational AI for robotics, has unveiled its first model, GENE-26.5, but also a demo showcasing a set of robotic hands performing complex tasks.

title: Tinder owner Match Group is slowing hiring to pay for its increased use of AI tools
description: Match Group said that it's slowing its hiring plans for the rest of the year because AI tools "cost a lot of money."

title: Apple to pay $250M to settle lawsuit over Siri’s delayed AI features
description: Apple has agreed to pay $250 million to settle a class action lawsuit for overpromising the arrival of Siri's AI features.

title: Ethos raises $22.75M from a16z for its expert network with voice onboarding
description: Ethos says it is onboarding 35,000 experts per week.

title: At TechCrunch Disrupt 2026, all your M&A questions will be answered
description: Leaders from Coinbase, M13, and Mignano Law Group talk about how M&A is an early-stage strategy at TechCrunch Disrupt 2026. Register to hear this live.

title: 3 days left to lock in 50% off a second ticket to TechCrunch Disrupt 2026
description: Three days left to lock in 50% off a second ticket to Disrupt 2026. Buy one TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 ticket, and get a second ticket at 50% off. Gain more visibility in the tech industry. Offer ends May 8 at 11:59 p.m. PT.

title: AI boom pushes Samsung to $1T
description: Samsung crossed the $1 trillion valuation mark after shares surged on AI-driven chip demand, making it only the second Asian company after TSMC to hit the milestone.

title: Peter Sarlin’s QuTwo reaches $380M valuation in angel round
description: Enterprise AI will be QuTwo’s bread and butter. “AI is the north star that we will continue to aim for. Quantum is just a new type of compute,” said Sarlin, who is adamant that QuTwo is an AI company.

title: Marc Lore says that AI will soon enable anyone to open a restaurant
description: Wonder wants to turn its robotic kitchens into AI-powered “restaurant factories,” letting anyone spin up a virtual food brand with a prompt.

title: SAP bets $1.16B on 18-month-old German AI lab and says yes to NemoClaw
description: SAP plans to buy German AI startup Prior Labs and invest heavily in it. It is also prohibiting customers' agents use to a select few like Nvidia's NemoClaw.

title: Altara secures $7M to bridge the data gap that’s slowing down physical sciences
description: Altara’s AI aims to diagnose failures and help speed up R&D by unifying data siloed across spreadsheets and legacy systems.

title: Apple plans to make iOS 27 a Choose Your Own Adventure of AI models
description: With Apple's latest operating system updates, users will reportedly have their pick of which third-party AI models they want to use for a host of tasks.

title: ASML CEO Christophe Fouquet on his company’s monopoly: no one is coming for us
description: Christophe Fouquet, who became ASML's CEO in 2024 after more than a decade at the company, sat down with this editor on the rooftop deck of his Beverly Hills hotel Tuesday morning ahead of his appearance at the Milken Institute Global Conference. Dressed in a blue suit and white shirt, he was relaxed — even when the conversation turned to the rivals.
